nodes.label: AutoGen
nodes.summary: AutoGen is an open-source framework for building LLM applications via multi-agent conversations, allowing for customizable and conversable agents.

nodes.label: Conversable Agent
nodes.summary: A conversable agent is an entity that can send and receive messages to interact with other agents, using LLMs, human inputs, or tools.

nodes.label: Multi-Agent Conversations
nodes.summary: Multi-agent conversations refer to communication among multiple agents to accomplish tasks through modular interactions.

nodes.label: Conversation Programming
nodes.summary: Conversation Programming is a paradigm in AutoGen that defines agent interactions and flows using natural and programming languages.

nodes.label: AssistantAgent
nodes.summary: AssistantAgent is a pre-configured conversable agent in AutoGen, acting primarily as an AI assistant using LLMs.

nodes.label: UserProxyAgent
nodes.summary: UserProxyAgent is a pre-configured agent in AutoGen, capable of integrating human input or executing code.

nodes.label: Retrieval-Augmented Generation
nodes.summary: An approach for enhancing LLM capabilities by incorporating external documents for more effective output.

nodes.label: GroupChatManager
nodes.summary: GroupChatManager is an AutoGen component that manages dynamic multi-agent conversations by selecting speakers and broadcasting responses.

nodes.label: Ahmed Awadallah
nodes.summary: Ahmed Awadallah is one of the authors of the paper, contributing to the development and research of the AutoGen framework.

nodes.label: Qingyun Wu
nodes.summary: Qingyun Wu is an author of the paper, helping in the development and conceptualization of AutoGen.

nodes.label: Human-in-the-loop
nodes.summary: An approach where human input is integrated into the automated process, allowing for human oversight and decision-making.

nodes.label: LLM
nodes.summary: Large Language Models are neural network-based models that process and generate human-like text, used as the backbone for conversable agents.

nodes.label: Math Problem Solving
nodes.summary: An application of AutoGen involving solving mathematical problems using LLM-powered agents.

nodes.label: ALFWorld
nodes.summary: A testbed for evaluating conversational agent systems in reasoning and task performance.
